steps.title: Find the exponential pattern
steps.content: Use the exponential form $h(x)=a\cdot b^x$. The table shows $h(0)=1.25$, so $a=1.25$. Since $h(2)=1.80$, the multiplier must satisfy $$1.25b^2=1.80.$$ This gives $b^2=1.44$, so $b=1.20$.

steps.title: Check the model quickly
steps.content: The coefficient is correct because $h(0)=1.25(1.20)^0=1.25$. The base is correct because $h(2)=1.25(1.20)^2=1.25(1.44)=1.80$, matching the table.

steps.title: Check each choice
steps.content:
• $h(x) = 1.20(0.25)^x$ has the wrong initial value and the base $0.25 \lt 1$ would shrink the heights instead of growing them. Eliminated.
  • $h(x) = 1.20(1.25)^x$ has the wrong initial value $1.20$ at $x = 0$. Eliminated.
  • $h(x) = 1.25(0.20)^x$ starts at the right value but the base $0.20$ would make heights collapse toward zero. Eliminated.
  • The matching choice: $h(x) = 1.25(1.20)^x$ gives $h(0) = 1.25$ and $h(2) = 1.25(1.44) = 1.80$, matching the table. Matches.
D is correct.
